skill.name: generate-image
skill.description: Generate and edit general-purpose images through OpenRouter image models such as Gemini and FLUX. The skill provides a Python helper for prompts, input images, model selection, and output file paths.
skill.summary: Generate or edit images with OpenRouter models from text prompts or input images.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified network access, API key handling, .env file reads, and local file writes in an executable Python script. These behaviors are aligned with an OpenRouter image generation tool, but they require a marketplace warning because prompts, input images, and credentials are processed by a third-party API. The base64 and weak-crypto detections are false positives for image encoding, and the external command detections in SKILL.md are mostly shell examples rather than hidden execution.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Transmission of User Prompts and Images
medium_findings.description: The script sends the user prompt and, in edit mode, a base64-encoded input image to the OpenRouter chat completions endpoint. This is expected for the skill, but users must understand that prompt content and source images leave the local workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network request is explicit and includes user-controlled prompt content plus optional image data. The endpoint is a legitimate OpenRouter API endpoint, so this is a privacy and disclosure risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: API Key Handling Through .env and Command Line
medium_findings.description: The script searches the current directory and parent directories for a .env file containing OPENROUTER_API_KEY and also accepts an API key through --api-key. This is legitimate credential use for OpenRouter, but broad parent-directory scanning can collect a key outside the immediate project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads .env files and passes the API key as an Authorization bearer token. The purpose is documented and consistent with OpenRouter usage, but credential access is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Read and Write Paths
medium_findings.description: The --input option reads an image file selected by the user, and the --output option writes decoded image data to a user-selected path. This is expected behavior, but it can overwrite local files if a user provides a sensitive output path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write paths are user-controlled command-line arguments with no path restriction or overwrite confirmation. This is normal for a CLI helper but remains a local filesystem risk.

low_findings.title: Base64 Image Encoding Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged base64 use as weak cryptography, but the code uses base64 only to encode input images and decode generated image data. No password hashing, encryption, signing, or security boundary depends on base64.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is image data transport, not cryptographic protection. I found no evidence that base64 is presented as encryption or used for secret storage.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as External Execution
low_findings.description: Many external-command detections occur inside SKILL.md examples showing how a user can run the helper script. These examples are documentation, not code that automatically executes commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced shell examples and parameter documentation. They do not create automatic command execution or a command injection vector by themselves.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: The reviewed skill files describe image generation usage and do not contain instructions to override security analysis, skip review, impersonate system authority, or ignore previous instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: I reviewed the skill instructions and found no suspicious prompt injection phrases. This finding documents the absence of a semantic issue requested by the audit procedure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request With Bearer Token
dangerous_patterns.description: The script posts to OpenRouter with an Authorization bearer token. This is required for the service, but it combines network access and credential handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The request, endpoint, and Authorization header are directly visible in the code. The use is intentional but materially security-relevant.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent Directory .env Search
dangerous_patterns.description: The script searches from the current working directory through parent directories for .env and reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY. This may use credentials from a broader workspace than the caller expects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The loop over current_dir.parents confirms broad .env discovery. It is not hidden, but it expands credential scope.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unrestricted Output File Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes decoded image bytes to the output path supplied by the caller. It does not check for existing files or restrict writes to a generated-assets directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write behavior is explicit and the output path is user-controlled. Risk depends on how a user invokes the CLI, so confidence is high for the pattern and moderate for exploitability.

content.value_statement: Teams need quick visual assets without building a custom image pipeline. This skill provides a ready Python workflow for generating and editing images through OpenRouter models.
actual_capabilities: Generates new images from text prompts using OpenRouter image models.
Edits an existing local image by sending the image and edit prompt to a supported model.
Lets users choose Gemini or FLUX model identifiers from the command line.
Saves the returned image data to a local PNG file path.
Looks for OPENROUTER_API_KEY in .env files or accepts it as a command-line option.
Documents examples for scientific, presentation, poster, documentation, and marketing visuals.
limitations: Requires a valid OpenRouter API key and network access.
Sends prompts and edited input images to OpenRouter for processing.
Does not validate model availability, cost, or current pricing before requests.
Does not provide technical diagram generation; the instructions recommend another skill for schematics.

use_cases.title: Create Presentation Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate background images, hero visuals, and conceptual illustrations for slides or posters.

use_cases.title: Produce Documentation Assets
use_cases.description: Create visual examples or supporting artwork for docs, websites, and internal guides.

use_cases.title: Iterate on Existing Images
use_cases.description: Use an input image and a targeted edit prompt to change colors, backgrounds, or selected visual elements.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a clean image of [subject] in [style]. Use [mood] lighting and save it as [file name].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Presentation Asset
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a professional visual for [topic]. It should support a presentation about [audience or goal] and leave space for title text.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit [input image path] by changing [specific element] to [desired change]. Preserve the main subject and save the result as [output path].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [asset description] with [model name]. Optimize for [quality, speed, or cost] and use [output path] for the final image.

output_examples.input: A prompt asks for a modern office collaboration image for documentation.
output_examples.output: The skill sends the prompt to the selected OpenRouter image model and saves the returned PNG as a local documentation asset.

output_examples.input: A user provides a portrait image and asks to add sunglasses while preserving the person.
output_examples.output: The skill encodes the input image, sends it with the edit prompt, and writes the edited image to the chosen output path.

output_examples.input: A researcher needs a conceptual immunotherapy illustration for a slide deck.
output_examples.output: The skill generates a visual illustration from the scientific prompt and stores it in the requested slides or figures folder.

best_practices: Use a project-specific OpenRouter API key with the minimum required permissions.
Avoid sending confidential images, private documents, or regulated data to external models.
Choose explicit output paths in a generated-assets folder to avoid overwriting important files.
anti_patterns: Do not place broad production secrets in parent-directory .env files used by this script.
Do not use this skill for technical schematics, flowcharts, circuits, or precise diagrams.
Do not pass sensitive screenshots or customer images unless external processing is approved.

faq.question: Does this skill run locally only?
faq.answer: No. It runs a local Python script, but image generation and editing are processed by OpenRouter models.

faq.question: Which API key does it need?
faq.answer: It needs an OPENROUTER_API_KEY in a .env file, environment setup, or the --api-key command option.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It can send a supported local image with an edit prompt to a model that supports image editing.

faq.question: What output format does it save?
faq.answer: The script saves the returned image bytes to the output path, commonly as a PNG file.

faq.question: Is it suitable for private images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Input images and prompts are sent to a third-party API, so private data needs approval first.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams or schematics?
faq.answer: It is intended for general visuals. The skill instructions direct technical diagrams to a separate schematics skill.
